title: An Egerton Marriage Settlement, dated 10 Henry VI
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository representation of University of Michigan CME00127; matching identifiers and content establish version identity, but the exact import or copy direction is undocumented. Its immediate source is a discontiguous selection from William Beamont's 1864 article: the article subtitle on printed p. 183 is relocated as the XML HEAD, and Beamont's editorial headnote plus his full-length transcription occupy separate footnote zones on printed pp. 197-199. The encoding omits the surrounding article analysis, including Beamont's argument that the settlement was spurious. Beamont says he transcribed an original then in Sir Philip Grey Egerton's possession and published a one-quarter-size photographic view of one visible document surface. The original's current holding is unknown. A complete 22-page ADS article PDF and a complete 653-page Internet Archive volume scan, OCR, scan metadata, and item metadata are cached locally with fixity; none is a complete physical-object facsimile of the original deed.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00127.xml. The U-M encoding, 1864 article, original document, published photographic plate, and modern digital scans are separate entities.
The XML HEAD is the article subtitle printed on p. 183, not a heading visible on p. 197. Its placement after PB N=197 is an encoding arrangement and does not establish a p. 197 source locus.

The encoded prose is spatially zoned rather than page-complete. On printed pp. 197-199 Beamont's main analysis continues above or alongside the footnote transcription; CME00127 retains only the footnote headnote and quoted document text.

On printed p. 199 the encoded transcription occupies a separate mid-page block and ends with 'Yeven the yere and day abovesaide' before the pedigree. Beamont's spurious-settlement conclusion in the surrounding main text is not encoded.

Beamont describes the document as an indenture, dates its formula to 20 March 1432, notes one former seal position but no surviving seal, and argues that it was a contemporary forgery. These are attributed 1864 judgments, not silently adopted physical or authenticity facts.

The published photographic plate appears to reproduce one long document surface in a single reduced view. It does not show a reverse or establish complete physical-object coverage, preservation-master resolution, color fidelity, or the object's present condition.

The last item-specific holding statement found is Beamont's 1864 report that the original belonged to Sir Philip Grey Egerton. Cheshire Archives' current Egerton of Oulton collection DEO is contextual only; no exact current catalog record for this settlement was found on 2026-07-13.

The repository root license at the pinned CME revision is the software-worded Unlicense. Its application to corpus XML, markup, and data is not explicit, so the repository-artifact rights status remains unknown rather than being broadened from the underlying public-domain text and edition.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00127

notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00127; the file is 7,360 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME00127 electronic text
alternate_labels: An Egerton marriage settlement, dated 10 Henry VI
entities.description: A public U-M Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item encoding Beamont's supplied article subtitle, the editorial headnote introducing the original and plate, and the quoted Middle English settlement. It omits the article's main-text discussion and the photographic plate itself.

entities.label: William Beamont, On Marriage Contracts (1864)
alternate_labels: On marriage contracts, with remarks on an Egerton marriage settlement, dated 10 Henry VI
entities.description: A twenty-page antiquarian article surveying marriage contracts and discussing an Egerton settlement. Beamont prints a full-length transcription in an extended footnote on pp. 197-199 and a reduced photographic plate between pp. 196 and 197. His main text argues that the dated settlement is spurious and possibly a contemporary forgery; that analysis is outside CME00127.

bibliographic.citation: William Beamont, 'On Marriage Contracts, with Remarks on an Egerton Marriage Settlement, dated 10 Henry VI,' Journal of the Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester, old series 2 (1864): 183-202.
bibliographic.title: On Marriage Contracts, with Remarks on an Egerton Marriage Settlement, dated 10 Henry VI
bibliographic.container_title: Journal of the Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester
bibliographic.publisher: Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester

notes: CME00127 is not a complete encoding of this article. It selects the p. 183 subtitle and the footnote headnote/transcription zones on pp. 197-199.

entities.label: Original Egerton marriage-settlement indenture seen by Beamont
alternate_labels: Ralph Egerton's part of the indenture (Beamont's identification)
entities.description: The physical document that Beamont said was in Sir Philip Grey Egerton's possession in 1864. Beamont describes it as an indenture dated by its text to the Feast of St Cuthbert in 10 Henry VI, interprets that date as 20 March 1432, reports one former seal position but no surviving seal, and identifies it as Ralph Egerton's part. Its present location, shelfmark, support, reverse, and condition have not been established.

date_statements.label: Date stated by the document and interpreted by Beamont
date_statements.value: Feast of St Cuthbert, 10 Henry VI (20 March 1432)
date_statements.notes: The formula and Beamont's calendar conversion are direct evidence for the date claimed by the document, not proof of its actual execution date; Beamont disputes its authenticity.

date_statements.label: Item-specific historical possession
date_statements.notes: Beamont explicitly says the original was then in Sir Philip Grey Egerton's possession.

physical_description.extent: One long indenture document; one visible surface reproduced in the 1864 plate
physical_description.format: Indenture or deed, according to Beamont
notes: Beamont says no word was bisected in forming the indenture's top edge.
Beamont reports one former seal position but says no seal remained.
The reduced plate is insufficient to establish support, dimensions, reverse contents, complete physical coverage, color, or present condition.
Sir Philip Grey Egerton's 1864 possession is historical provenance, not a current holding.
Beamont's description of the settlement as spurious and likely a contemporary forgery is preserved as an attributed scholarly judgment.

entities.label: Beamont's one-quarter-size photographic plate of the Egerton settlement
entities.description: A rotated photographic illustration published between printed pp. 196 and 197. It appears to show one entire long document surface in a single view, at one-quarter the document's size according to Beamont. It does not reproduce a reverse or establish complete physical-object coverage.

entities.label: ADS digital scan of Beamont's 1864 article
entities.description: A complete 22-page PDF for the article, including printed pp. 183-202, a blank plate verso, and the photographic plate. The CME selection is discontiguous and spatially zoned within PDF pages 1 and 17-19; the plate is PDF page 16.

entities.label: Internet Archive digital facsimile of the Chester journal, old series volume 2
entities.description: A complete 653-page Google scan of an Oxford University copy of the 1864 bound volume, with complete provider OCR, scan metadata, and item metadata. Beamont's article occupies PDF pages 296-317; the plate is PDF page 311; the CME prose zones are on PDF pages 312-314, while the relocated subtitle is on PDF page 296.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images, plain-text OCR, scan-data XML, and item-metadata JSON
notes: The scan is complete relative to the bound volume represented by the provider item, not the original settlement as a physical object.

agents.name: Sir Philip de Malpas Grey Egerton
notes: Named by Beamont as the original document's owner in 1864; no current-holding claim is inferred.

agents.name: Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester

assertion.notes: Matching CME identifiers and complete represented content establish version identity, but no process documentation reviewed here proves the technical copy/import event or its direction.

Public U-M collection item route and stable name identifier

The direct PDF was retrieved without authentication. ADS identifies the applicable license as ADS Terms of Use and Access; local caching is a private research copy and does not imply redistribution permission.

notes: Preserves provider leaf, pagination, crop, and access-format metadata; it is not page-image coverage.

access.format: PDF, OCR text, scan-data XML, item-metadata JSON, and browser page images

access.access_method: No publicly cataloged complete physical-object facsimile was located; only Beamont's one-quarter-size view of one surface is known
notes: This is a dated negative search result, not a claim that no complete facsimile exists or can be obtained.
A complete surrogate would need every surviving side and physical surface, including reverse and seal area, with an identified current holder.

access.access_method: Ask Cheshire Archives whether the deed survives in an uncataloged or differently described Oulton-family series, and for advice on any later transfer
notes: DEO is the current Egerton of Oulton estate collection, but its public description begins in the sixteenth century and no exact record for this document was identified. This route does not assert that Cheshire Archives holds the original.
Cheshire Archives states that collection access is on hold until its new centers open in early 2027; enquiries remain the appropriate next step.

rights.component: The Middle English settlement text, whether created in the fifteenth century or no later than its 1864 publication
rights.basis: The anonymous historical text was published in full in 1864 and any copyright term has expired; this does not resolve ownership or access to the physical object or rights in later images and markup.

rights.component: Beamont's 1864 article, transcription, editorial prose, pedigree, and published plate
rights.basis: The article and plate were published in 1864 and are outside copyright term; provider-specific access terms for modern scan services remain separate.

rights.component: U-M-hosted CME00127 electronic item and encoded materials
rights.basis: The U-M CME collection says its materials are in the public domain in the United States and supplies the RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States statement.
notes: This current provider assertion is scoped to the U-M collection/item and does not license third-party scans or an unidentified physical document.

rights.component: Repository-specific XML file, markup, and revision metadata
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository contains an Unlicense text framed as applying to software. Its scope over corpus XML, markup, and data is not explicit enough to treat the complete artifact as unambiguously dedicated, although the underlying historical text and 1864 edition are public domain.

rights.component: ADS-hosted PDF scan and service-delivered derivatives
rights.basis: The article record applies ADS Terms of Use and Access. Those terms permit personal research or educational copies but require permission for other use and restrict onward supply; they do not provide an item-layer copyright determination for the scan distinct from the public-domain 1864 edition.
notes: The ignored local cache is a private research copy under the provider's stated terms and must not be treated as a redistributable corpus asset.

rights.component: Internet Archive and Google scan derivatives of the 1864 bound volume
rights.basis: The provider item exposes public downloads but its current metadata has no item-level rights, license URL, or possible-copyright-status field. Public availability is therefore not restated as an open license.
notes: The underlying 1864 edition is separately recorded as public domain.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A twenty-page article with a subtitle on p. 183, an extended footnote transcription on pp. 197-199, surrounding main-text analysis, pedigree, and plate
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding relocates the p. 183 subtitle after PB N=197 and retains only the p. 197 footnote headnote and the transcription zones through the mid-page endpoint on p. 199.
editorial_practices.description: CME00127 is a discontiguous editorial selection, not a complete article encoding and not a page-complete extraction of printed pp. 197-199.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire U-M CME00127 item relative to Beamont's article, pp. 183-202

editorial_practices.source_feature: A physical indenture with at least one photographed surface and an unrepresented reverse or other physical views
editorial_practices.representation: One long visible surface appears in a single monochrome printed view at one-quarter size.
editorial_practices.description: The complete published plate is only a partial reproduction of the physical document and cannot establish whole-object visual completeness.
editorial_practices.scope: Published plate relative to the original settlement as a physical object

evidence.citation: General Corpus repository copy of CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00127.xml at pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, BODY/DIV1, HEAD, HEADNOTE, two P elements, and PB N=197-199
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for identifiers, Middle English language, supplied title, editorial headnote, complete quoted settlement, typography, page milestones, and repository revision history.

evidence.citation: Local structural and textual audit of CME00127.xml against both complete 1864 source scans.
evidence.summary: Confirmed 7,360 bytes; one DIV1 typed marriage settlement, one HEAD, one HEADNOTE, two intellectual P elements, three PB elements, two italic HI elements, and four SUP elements. It also established that the HEAD derives from printed p. 183 while the prose derives from spatial footnote zones on pp. 197-199 and excludes the article's main analysis.
Contains the Unlicense text framed as applying to software; it does not explicitly resolve the license scope for XML, markup, data, underlying editions, or document images.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page and stable CME00127 identifier.
evidence.summary: Official evidence that the collection provides manually keyed, proofread public-domain texts, TEI-style markup, bulk XML, educational and research access, and a No Copyright - United States rights statement; the stable item identifier is CME00127.
notes: The exact item route was intermittently blocked to automated retrieval during review; the stable identifier and pinned repository file remained verifiable.

evidence.citation: William Beamont, 'On Marriage Contracts, with Remarks on an Egerton Marriage Settlement, dated 10 Henry VI,' Journal of the Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester, old series 2 (1864): 183-202.
evidence.locator: subtitle on p. 183/PDF p. 1; document discussion pp. 195-201; plate PDF p. 16; footnote headnote and transcription zones pp. 197-199/PDF pp. 17-19
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for article title, author, publication, transcription claim, 1864 possession by Sir Philip Grey Egerton, stated date conversion, missing seal report, one-quarter-size plate statement, textual content, shared-page source boundaries, and Beamont's attributed spurious/contemporary-forgery judgment.
notes: The SHA-256 is for the complete 22-page locally cached article PDF.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of the photographic plate in both complete 1864 scans.
evidence.locator: ADS PDF p. 16; Internet Archive volume PDF p. 311; unpaginated plate between printed pp. 196 and 197
evidence.summary: The rotated monochrome plate appears to show one complete long document surface in a single view. No reverse or separate physical views are present, so it supports one-surface partial coverage rather than complete physical-object coverage.

evidence.citation: Archaeology Data Service, article record 3204271 and Journal of the Chester Archaeological Society old-series volume 2 issue record 1147315.
evidence.summary: Official catalog evidence for Beamont's authorship, exact article title, old-series volume 2, 1864 publication, pp. 183-202, DOI, direct PDF, journal publisher/series, and ADS Terms of Use and Access license label.

evidence.citation: Archaeology Data Service, ADS Terms of Use and Access.
evidence.locator: Copyright Statement and Common Access Agreement, especially clauses governing personal research or educational copies and onward supply
evidence.summary: Current provider terms permit personal research or educational duplication, require acknowledgment, and require permission for other use; they restrict sale and onward supply. They govern use of the ADS-delivered PDF independently of the public-domain historical edition.

evidence.citation: Journal of the Architectural, Archaeological, and Historic Society for the County, City, and Neighbourhood of Chester, old series volume 2 (Chester, 1864), Internet Archive item journalarchitec00unkngoog.
evidence.locator: title page; Beamont article volume PDF pp. 296-317; subtitle p. 296; plate p. 311; encoded prose zones pp. 312-314
evidence.summary: Complete bound-volume evidence for the 1864 title-page imprint, article and plate context, exact discontiguous and spatially shared source boundaries, and the material omitted by CME00127.
notes: The SHA-256 is for the complete 653-page locally cached volume PDF.

evidence.summary: Records the provider identifier, volume title, year 1864, Oxford University source copy, Google source and scanning, exact derivative file inventory, and no item-level rights, license URL, or possible-copyright-status field.
notes: The hash is for the exact locally cached metadata JSON response.

evidence.citation: Cheshire Archives and Local Studies, Estate and Family Records page and current catalog searches for the Egerton of Oulton estate collection DEO.
evidence.summary: The official estate-records page identifies DEO as the Egerton of Oulton collection. The current collection description begins in the sixteenth century, and exact searches on 2026-07-13 for names, date formulas, incipit terms, and '10 Henry VI' found no item-level match. This supports an enquiry route and current-whereabouts uncertainty, not a transfer, loss, or destruction claim.
notes: Cheshire Archives currently says access to collections is on hold until new centers open in early 2027.

open_questions.question: What exact process and direction connect the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME XML artifact with the U-M CME00127 distribution?
next_steps: Locate repository import documentation, commit history, or U-M bulk-package provenance that identifies the transfer event and direction.
Until documented, retain version_of rather than copied_from.

open_questions.question: Where is the original Egerton marriage-settlement document now, and what current shelfmark or ownership history connects it to Sir Philip Grey Egerton's 1864 possession?
next_steps: Ask Cheshire Archives about uncataloged or differently described deeds in or related to Egerton of Oulton collection DEO, without assuming that DEO contains the item.
Trace Oulton Hall estate, sale, deposit, and family-paper records after Sir Philip Grey Egerton.
Once identified, obtain the exact current catalog record and provenance chain before adding a holding institution.

open_questions.question: Does current documentary, genealogical, palaeographic, or material evidence confirm, revise, or reject Beamont's 1864 conclusion that the settlement is a contemporary forgery rather than an authentic deed executed on 20 March 1432?
next_steps: Locate later scholarship or a modern catalog description that explicitly evaluates this document.
If the original is found, compare script, support, ink, indenture edge, seal area, and genealogical claims with securely dated Egerton deeds.
Keep the date formula and Beamont's authenticity judgment separately attributed until that review is possible.

open_questions.question: Can a complete modern reproduction of every surviving physical surface of the original be obtained?
next_steps: Identify the current holder before requesting images.
Request recto, verso, indenture edge, seal or seal-slot area, and overall color and scale targets rather than only the text-bearing face.
Retain Beamont's plate as one-surface partial coverage even if it appears to show that whole visible side.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded root Unlicense intentionally apply to the XML markup and data in CME00127?
next_steps: Seek a path-specific XML or data rights statement from the repository maintainer.
Until clarified, keep the repository-artifact status separate from the public-domain historical text and 1864 edition.
